stem: An office is being wired using a star topology.
model_answer: In a star topology every device connects directly to a central switch (or hub) with its own cable. All traffic passes through the central device, which forwards frames to the correct destination. If one cable or device fails only that node is affected; the rest of the network keeps working. Adding a new device is easy, but if the central switch fails the whole network goes down, and lots of cabling is needed.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Describe a star topology and give one advantage of using one.

mark_points.text: each device has its own cable to a central switch/hub
accepted_alternatives: all nodes connect to a central device

mark_points.text: traffic is sent via the central switch to the destination
advantage named: failure of one cable/device does not affect the rest of the network (or easy to add new devices, or better performance with a switch)
accepted_alternatives: one cable failure only affects that device
